title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her daughter's cancer battle.
Robertson's daughter, Emma, faced medulloblastoma, experiencing balance issues, requiring urgent surgery and extensive treatment.
The family temporarily relocated to Philadelphia for Emma's proton radiation therapy due to facility maintenance in Boston.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not debut against Tampa Bay Lightning as Bruins focus on securing a playoff spot.
Bruins head coach Marco Sturm cites Hagens's need to adapt to the team's system before debuting.
Coach Sturm suggests Hagens's presence motivates other forwards battling for lineup spots.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston-area billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers are featured on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list.
Forbes' historical self-made list includes Benjamin Franklin, Sheldon Adelson, and Red Sox legends Babe Ruth and Ted Williams from Massachusetts.
Forbes' rankings use a 'Self-Made Scores' scale measuring financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact, only including top scorers.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will take place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending in Copley Square.
This year's race features 30,000 participants from over 130 countries, with six start waves including elite and charity runners.
Live coverage will be available on WCVB Channel 5 and through the Very Local app, with additional regional simulcasts.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are adding former Titans and Giants defensive coordinator Shane Bowen as an analyst.
Shane Bowen previously worked with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at the Titans and Ohio State.
Bowen was fired from the Giants in November after several defensive struggles under interim coach Mike Kafka.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's public restrooms can be found in libraries, community centers, firehouses, hotels, and some restaurants.
An interactive map by the city's Age Strong Commission shows restroom locations across Boston neighborhoods.
Massachusetts law allows those with certain medical conditions access to employee-only restrooms in specific situations.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a freshman, claims Masconomet school administrators failed to respond to harassment reports, including threats of sexual violence.
Teachers at Masconomet expressed concern over gender discrimination and inadequate administrative actions against athletes involved in bullying.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes allegations of improper responses, citing privacy regulations as a barrier to detailing the school's actions.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A report shows Massachusetts drivers paid over $1 million extra for gasoline since the Iran conflict began in late February.
Massachusetts home oil costs rose 37.9%, with average families spending over $200 more for home heating since the conflict began.
U.S. Sen. Ed Markey highlighted an annual increase of $1,096 in gas costs for families with two cars due to the conflict.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Canoe River Campground in Mansfield offers 300 sites and has availability during World Cup matches, with rates starting at $51.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver provides over 200 sites, including glamping options, during the World Cup dates.
Boston hotels raise prices during World Cup; FIFA reduces bookings due to inflated original estimates in host cities.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, a stroke survivor, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Rafferty suffered a stroke in 2019 that left him paralyzed on the left side, but he recovered with Spaulding's help.
Tim completed his first Boston Marathon in 2021, just two years after his stroke, and is running again this year.
